prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which is commonly used for stock price forecasting. Starting with the current price of $384.47, we project 18 trading days (approximately 0.0714 years) into the future. We assume an annualized drift (average return) of 12% and an annualized volatility of 25%, parameters informed by MSFT's historical performance and general market conditions. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution at the end of the 18-day period. The confidence intervals are calculated using the standard deviation of the log returns over the specified timeframe, with corresponding Z-scores for each confidence level (0.674 for 50%, 1.282 for 80%, and 1.645 for 90%). These predictions are theoretical and highly sensitive to the assumed drift and volatility; actual market movements, influenced by unforeseen news or events, can deviate significantly from this model.
